title: hoya odorata subsp. antoinsensis
intro: Hoya odorata subsp. antoinsensis, also known as the waxflower or fragrant hoya, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as hypertension and arthritis. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, offering a range of health benefits. Hoya odorata subsp. antoinsensis is often prepared as a tea or tincture for consumption, and its flowers and leaves can also be used in aromatherapy to promote a sense of calm and well-being.
